title: Sour Crispy British Mirin-Hon Welsh Rarebit
description: A twist on the classic Welsh rarebit, this recipe incorporates the sweet and sour flavors of mirin-hon. Serve as a snack or dessert.
instructions: In a medium saucepan, whisk together butter, flour, mirin-hon, and english mustard over low heat, stirring constantly.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook for 2 minutes, until thickened.
Remove the saucepan from the heat and stir in the grated cheddar cheese until melted.
Stir in the beaten egg and season with salt to taste.
Pour the cheese sauce into a serving dish and set aside.
To make the crispy topping, whisk together soda water and bread crumbs in a separate bowl until well combined.
Pour the topping mixture over the cheese sauce and use a spatula to spread it evenly.
Place the dish under the broiler and cook for 2-3 minutes, until the topping is golden brown and crispy.
Remove the dish from the oven and let it cool for 1-2 minutes before serving.
Split the rarebit into individual portions and serve immediately.
Garnish with fresh herbs, if desired.
Serve warm or chilled, depending on your preference.
Store any leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.
Reheat in the microwave or oven until warm and crispy.
Note: For an extra crispy topping, try baking the rarebit in a preheated oven at 375°F (190°C) for 5-7 minutes, or until golden brown.
If you prefer a crisper topping, you can try broiling the rarebit for an additional 1-2 minutes, watching closely to avoid burning.
